ticker_items: First 6 days of Iran war cost $11.3 billion, Pentagon tells Congress
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei injured in initial US-Israel airstrikes
Saudi Arabia intercepts drone headed to Shaybah oil field
Iran threatens to target US and Israeli economic institutions
Developed nations release record emergency oil reserves amid war

middle_east.description: Saudi Arabia intercepted Iranian drone headed to Shaybah oil field
Israeli strikes targeted 10 Hezbollah targets in Beirut

middle_east.text: under no circumstances can we participate in the World Cup
middle_east.speaker: Ahmad Donyamali
middle_east.title: Iran's minister of sport and youth

observers.description: Putin is profiting from rising oil prices amid the Iran war. Russia is no longer the lead disruptor of the postwar global order as Trump's conflict takes center stage.
China is watching warily as war spreads across the Middle East. Beijing is simultaneously strengthening its influence over North Korea with new infrastructure projects along their shared border.
Australia is helping the US in the Iran conflict by sending troops to the Middle East. Senator Nick McKim warned that Prime Minister Albanese has 'hitched' Australia to the conflict.
South Africa is navigating an awkward diplomatic dance between Washington and Tehran during the Iran war. Pretoria is attempting to balance its relationships with both sides of the conflict.

financial.notes: Developed nations agreed to release record emergency oil reserves to counter rising prices from the Iran war.

update_log.text: Pentagon reveals first 6 days of Iran war cost $11.3 billion
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei injured in initial US-Israel airstrikes
Saudi Arabia intercepts Iranian drone targeting Shaybah oil field
Israel strikes 10 Hezbollah targets in Beirut
Developed nations release record emergency oil reserves amid rising prices
